help_job_group: Optional label for filtering and bulk enable/disable in the job list. Does not affect scheduling.
cron_help_format: Five space-separated fields: minute · hour · day of month · month · day of week. Asterisk (*) means any value. All times are UTC.
cron_help_examples: Popular examples (click Use to fill the field):
help_enabled: While disabled, the job will not run on schedule. You can still run it manually with Run now.
help_schedule_intro: Controls when the scheduler runs this job automatically.
help_schedule_interval: Fixed interval between runs: m = minutes, h = hours, d = days (e.g. 5m, 2h, 1d).
help_schedule_onetime: The job runs once at the selected time and is not scheduled again.
help_one_time_picker: Pick date and time in your local timezone. The UTC value stored on the server is shown below.
error_one_time_required: Select a run date and time
error_one_time_future: Run time must be in the future
help_fetch_section: Optional first step: HTTP request to an external service. The response body (usually JSON) is passed to transform or send.
help_fetch_headers: JSON object of headers, e.g. {"Authorization": "Bearer YOUR_TOKEN", "Accept": "application/json"}
help_fetch_body: Request body for POST. Leave empty for GET.
help_transform_section: Optional step between fetch and send. Script runs in a sandboxed JS engine: variable input holds the fetch JSON; return value is sent to the send step.
help_transform_rules: Only JSON manipulation is allowed (map, filter, formatting). No filesystem, network, or environment access.
transform_ex_filter_desc: Keep only items where active === true.
transform_ex_wrap_desc: Add source label and timestamp around the data.
transform_ex_pick_desc: Build a flat object from selected input fields.
help_send_section: Optional final step: POST or PUT the result to another URL. If transform is off, the fetch response is sent.
help_send_body: Request body template. Placeholder {{payload}} is replaced with JSON after transform (or after fetch).
help_retry_section: Retries on failed request processing: if fetch, transform, or send fails (network error, HTTP 4xx/5xx, invalid JSON), the job runs again until retries are exhausted.
help_retry_enabled: When enabled, a failed run is retried instead of marking the job failed immediately.
help_max_retries: Extra attempts after the first failure (0 = no retries, single attempt only).
help_retry_interval: Seconds to wait between retry attempts.
log_details_preview_info: SQLite stores only response_preview — a short snippet for the UI (up to {{max}} bytes, see AJS_LOG_RESPONSE_PREVIEW_MAX_BYTES). The fetch/transform/send pipeline uses the full response body; it is not saved whole in the database.
log_details_truncated_notice: Showing the stored snippet only: the original response exceeded the limit ({{max}} bytes) and was truncated when the log row was written.
select_job_logs: Select a job to view logs
